revision.text:
'''ニサンザイ古墳''' # [[土師ニサンザイ古墳]] - 大阪府堺市百舌鳥西之町の古墳 # [[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]] - 大阪府泉南郡岬町淡輪の古墳
[[Category:日本の古墳|にさんさい こふん]] {{aimai}}

'''ミサンザイ古墳'''（みさんざいこふん）とは、日本の古墳のいくつかに用いられている名称。 ミサンザイとは貴人の墓を意味する『みささぎ（御陵）』が転訛したものといわれる。[[宮内庁]]により多くが歴代の天皇陵に比定され[[書陵部]]により管轄されている。
*[[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] （[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]） 別名「百舌鳥耳原南陵」（もずのみみはらのみなみのみささぎ）」。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀前半の造営と考えられている。第17代[[履中天皇]]陵に比定されている。 *[[岡ミサンザイ古墳]] （大阪府[[藤井寺市]]） 別名「惠我長野西陵（えがのながののにしのみささぎ）」。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀後半の造営と考えられている。第14代[[仲哀天皇]]陵に比定されている。 *[[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町） 別名「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ）」。5世紀末から6世紀初頭の造営と考えられている。第28代[[宣化天皇]]陵に比定されている。 *[[神武天皇#陵墓・霊廟|山本ミサンザイ古墳]]（奈良県橿原市畝傍町） 別名「畝傍山東北陵（うねびのやまのうしとらのすみのみささぎ）」。元は神武田と呼ばれた場所にあった小さな塚を、幕末期からの調査によって初代[[神武天皇]]陵に比定した後、大正期にかけて現在の規模まで改修して陵墓としたもの。
[[Category:日本の古墳|みさんさいこふん]]

'''ミサンザイ古墳'''（みさんざいこふん）とは、日本の古墳のいくつかに用いられている名称。 ミサンザイとは貴人の墓を意味する『みささぎ（御陵）』が転訛したものといわれる。[[宮内庁]]により多くが歴代の天皇陵に比定され[[書陵部]]により管轄されている。
*[[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] （[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]） 別名「百舌鳥耳原南陵」（もずのみみはらのみなみのみささぎ）」。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀前半の造営と考えられている。第17代[[履中天皇]]陵に比定されている。 *[[岡ミサンザイ古墳]] （大阪府[[藤井寺市]]） 別名「惠我長野西陵（えがのながののにしのみささぎ）」。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀後半の造営と考えられている。第14代[[仲哀天皇]]陵に比定されている。 *[[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町） 別名「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ）」。5世紀末から6世紀初頭の造営と考えられている。第28代[[宣化天皇]]陵に比定されている。 *[[神武天皇#陵墓・霊廟|山本ミサンザイ古墳]]（奈良県橿原市畝傍町） 別名「畝傍山東北陵（うねびのやまのうしとらのすみのみささぎ）」。元は神武田と呼ばれた場所にあった小さな塚を、幕末期からの調査によって初代[[神武天皇]]陵に比定した後、大正期にかけて現在の規模まで改修して陵墓としたもの。
{{DEFAULTSORT:みさんさいこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳|みさんさいこふん]]

'''ミサンザイ古墳'''（みさんざいこふん）とは、日本の古墳のいくつかに用いられている名称。 ミサンザイとは貴人の墓を意味する『みささぎ（御陵）』が転訛したものといわれる。[[宮内庁]]により多くが歴代の天皇陵に比定され[[書陵部]]により管轄されている。
* [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] （[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]） *: 別名「百舌鳥耳原南陵」（もずのみみはらのみなみのみささぎ）」。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀前半の造営と考えられている。第17代[[履中天皇]]陵に比定されている。 * [[岡ミサンザイ古墳]] （大阪府[[藤井寺市]]） *: 別名「惠我長野西陵（えがのながののにしのみささぎ）」。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀後半の造営と考えられている。第14代[[仲哀天皇]]陵に比定されている。 * [[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町） *: 別名「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ）」。5世紀末から6世紀初頭の造営と考えられている。第28代[[宣化天皇]]陵に比定されている。 * [[四条ミサンザイ古墳]]（奈良県橿原市畝傍町） *: 別名「畝傍山東北陵（うねびのやまのうしとらのすみのみささぎ）」。考古名として「山本ミサンザイ古墳」「神武田古墳」などがある。元は神武田と呼ばれた場所にあった小さな塚を、幕末期からの調査によって初代[[神武天皇]]陵に比定した後、大正期にかけて現在の規模まで改修して陵墓としたもの。第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵とともに、[[藤原京]]建設の際に削平された[[四条古墳群]]との関係性が指摘されている<ref>{{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/event/atrium/2010/shijyo-site.pdf 「四条遺跡 -大和高田バイパス建設関連の発掘調査とその成果-」]}}（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] アトリウム展示資料）、2010年。|ref={{Harvid|橿考研|2010年}}}}</ref>。
== 参照 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:みさんさいこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳|みさんさいこふん]]

'''ミサンザイ古墳'''（みさんざいこふん）とは、日本の古墳のいくつかに用いられている名称。 ミサンザイとは貴人の墓を意味する『みささぎ（御陵）』が転訛したものといわれる。[[宮内庁]]により多くが歴代の天皇陵に比定され[[書陵部]]により管轄されている。
* [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] （[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]） *: 別名「百舌鳥耳原南陵」（もずのみみはらのみなみのみささぎ）」。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀前半の造営と考えられている。第17代[[履中天皇]]陵に比定されている。 * [[岡ミサンザイ古墳]] （大阪府[[藤井寺市]]） *: 別名「惠我長野西陵（えがのながののにしのみささぎ）」。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀後半の造営と考えられている。第14代[[仲哀天皇]]陵に比定されている。 * [[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町） *: 別名「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ）」。5世紀末から6世紀初頭の造営と考えられている。第28代[[宣化天皇]]陵に比定されている。 * [[四条ミサンザイ古墳]]（奈良県橿原市畝傍町） *: 別名「畝傍山東北陵（うねびのやまのうしとらのすみのみささぎ）」。考古名として「山本ミサンザイ古墳」「神武田古墳」などがある。元は神武田と呼ばれた場所にあった小さな塚を、幕末期からの調査によって初代[[神武天皇]]陵に比定した後、大正期にかけて現在の規模まで改修して陵墓としたもの。第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵とともに、[[藤原京]]建設の際に削平された[[四条古墳群]]との関係性が指摘されている<ref>{{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/event/atrium/2010/shijyo-site.pdf 「四条遺跡 -大和高田バイパス建設関連の発掘調査とその成果-」]}}（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] アトリウム展示資料）、2010年。|ref={{Harvid|橿考研|2010年}}}}</ref>。 *:[[安本美典]]は、原初の神武陵を『[[古事記]]』の「御陵（みはか）は畝傍山の北の方の白檮の尾の上（かしのおのえ）にあり。」、『[[日本書紀]]』「畝傍山の東北の陵に葬りまつる。」を史料として畝傍山山麓にあったとし、さらに、『陵墓史』<ref>竹口英斎『陵墓史』[[寛政]]6年([[1794年]])。</ref>に、畝傍山の北東陵に「丸山」という名の地名があり、ここに「生玉神社」と呼ばれる祠があったこと、そばに「加志（かし）」という「橿」に通じる地名があったこと、またそばに「洞」という集落があって住人が神武陵の[[陵戸]]と伝わっている<ref>蒲生君平『山陵志』「洞村のことを相伝うるに、その民はもと神武陵の守戸なり。およそ守陵の戸は、みな賎種。もと罪隷をもって没入したる者は、郷にならばず」</ref>ことなどを根拠に、ここが真の神武陵だったと断定している。
== 参照 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:みさんさいこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳|みさんさいこふん]]

revision.comment: 宮内庁による陵墓は「治定」が使用される。
revision.text:
'''ミサンザイ古墳'''（みさんざいこふん）とは、日本の古墳のいくつかに用いられている名称。 ミサンザイとは貴人の墓を意味する『みささぎ（御陵）』が転訛したものといわれる。[[宮内庁]]により多くが歴代の天皇陵に比定され[[書陵部]]により管轄されている。
* [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] （[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]） *: 別名「百舌鳥耳原南陵」（もずのみみはらのみなみのみささぎ）」。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀前半の造営と考えられている。宮内庁により第17代[[履中天皇]]陵に治定されている。 * [[岡ミサンザイ古墳]] （大阪府[[藤井寺市]]） *: 別名「惠我長野西陵（えがのながののにしのみささぎ）」。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀後半の造営と考えられている。宮内庁により第14代[[仲哀天皇]]陵に治定されている。 * [[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町） *: 別名「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ）」。5世紀末から6世紀初頭の造営と考えられている。宮内庁により第28代[[宣化天皇]]陵に治定されている。 * [[四条ミサンザイ古墳]]（奈良県橿原市畝傍町） *: 別名「畝傍山東北陵（うねびのやまのうしとらのすみのみささぎ）」。考古名として「山本ミサンザイ古墳」「神武田古墳」などがある。元は神武田と呼ばれた場所にあった小さな塚を、幕末期からの調査によって初代[[神武天皇]]陵に比定した後、大正期にかけて現在の規模まで改修して陵墓としたもの。第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵とともに、[[藤原京]]建設の際に削平された[[四条古墳群]]との関係性が指摘されている<ref>{{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/event/atrium/2010/shijyo-site.pdf 「四条遺跡 -大和高田バイパス建設関連の発掘調査とその成果-」]}}（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] アトリウム展示資料）、2010年。|ref={{Harvid|橿考研|2010年}}}}</ref>。 *:[[安本美典]]は、原初の神武陵を『[[古事記]]』の「御陵（みはか）は畝傍山の北の方の白檮の尾の上（かしのおのえ）にあり。」、『[[日本書紀]]』「畝傍山の東北の陵に葬りまつる。」を史料として畝傍山山麓にあったとし、さらに、『陵墓史』<ref>竹口英斎『陵墓史』[[寛政]]6年([[1794年]])。</ref>に、畝傍山の北東陵に「丸山」という名の地名があり、ここに「生玉神社」と呼ばれる祠があったこと、そばに「加志（かし）」という「橿」に通じる地名があったこと、またそばに「洞」という集落があって住人が神武陵の[[陵戸]]と伝わっている<ref>蒲生君平『山陵志』「洞村のことを相伝うるに、その民はもと神武陵の守戸なり。およそ守陵の戸は、みな賎種。もと罪隷をもって没入したる者は、郷にならばず」</ref>ことなどを根拠に、ここが真の神武陵だったと断定している。

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]][[ミサンザイ古墳]]へのリダイレクト
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[ミサンザイ古墳]]

'''ミサンザイ古墳'''（みさんざいこふん）とは、日本の古墳のいくつかに用いられている名称。
ミサンザイとは貴人の墓を意味する『みささぎ（御陵）』が転訛したものといわれる。河内地方では'''ニサンザイ'''とも呼ばれている。[[宮内庁]]により多くが歴代の天皇陵や陵墓参考地に治定・比定され、[[書陵部]]により管轄されている。
* [[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]]（たんのわ -） （大阪府[[泉南郡]][[岬町]]淡輪） *: 大阪府最南端で大阪湾に面した地にある。5世紀後半の造営とされ、宮内庁により「宇度墓（うどのはか）陵墓参考地」とされている。推定される埋葬者は第11代[[垂仁天皇]]皇子の[[五十瓊敷入彦命]]が候補にあがっている。 * [[岡ミサンザイ古墳]] （大阪府[[藤井寺市]]） *: 別名「惠我長野西陵（えがのながののにしのみささぎ）」。[[古市古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀後半の造営と考えられている。宮内庁により第14代[[仲哀天皇]]陵に治定されている。 * [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] （かみいしづ -）（[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]） *: 別名「百舌鳥耳原南陵」（もずのみみはらのみなみのみささぎ）」。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属する古墳で、5世紀前半の造営と考えられている。宮内庁により第17代[[履中天皇]]陵に治定されている。 * [[土師ニサンザイ古墳]] （はぜ -）（大阪府[[堺市]]百舌鳥西之町） *: [[百舌鳥古墳群]]の南東端に位置する。第18代[[反正天皇]]古墳は、同古墳群の[[田出井山古墳]]に治定されているが、こちらが全国でも8位の規模の大きさがあり、これに比定する説がある。造営は5世紀後半とされ、宮内庁は「東百舌鳥陵墓参考地」に治定している。 * [[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町） *: 別名「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ）」。5世紀末から6世紀初頭の造営と考えられている。宮内庁により第28代[[宣化天皇]]陵に治定されている。 * [[四条ミサンザイ古墳]]（奈良県橿原市畝傍町） *: 別名「畝傍山東北陵（うねびのやまのうしとらのすみのみささぎ）」。考古名として「山本ミサンザイ古墳」「神武田古墳」などがある。元は神武田と呼ばれた場所にあった小さな塚を、幕末期からの調査によって初代[[神武天皇]]陵に比定した後、大正期にかけて現在の規模まで改修して陵墓としたもの。第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵とともに、[[藤原京]]建設の際に削平された[[四条古墳群]]との関係性が指摘されている<ref>{{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/event/atrium/2010/shijyo-site.pdf 「四条遺跡 -大和高田バイパス建設関連の発掘調査とその成果-」]}}（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] アトリウム展示資料）、2010年。|ref={{Harvid|橿考研|2010年}}}}</ref>。 *:[[安本美典]]は、原初の神武陵を『[[古事記]]』の「御陵（みはか）は畝傍山の北の方の白檮の尾の上（かしのおのえ）にあり。」、『[[日本書紀]]』「畝傍山の東北の陵に葬りまつる。」を史料として畝傍山山麓にあったとし、さらに、『陵墓史』<ref>竹口英斎『陵墓史』[[寛政]]6年([[1794年]])。</ref>に、畝傍山の北東陵に「丸山」という名の地名があり、ここに「生玉神社」と呼ばれる祠があったこと、そばに「加志（かし）」という「橿」に通じる地名があったこと、またそばに「洞」という集落があって住人が神武陵の[[陵戸]]と伝わっている<ref>蒲生君平『山陵志』「洞村のことを相伝うるに、その民はもと神武陵の守戸なり。およそ守陵の戸は、みな賎種。もと罪隷をもって没入したる者は、郷にならばず」</ref>ことなどを根拠に、ここが真の神武陵だったと断定している。
== 参照 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:みさんさいこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳|みさんさいこふん]]

revision.comment: 宇度墓は陵墓参考地ではなく公式墓。併せて体裁を揃えます。そもそも論で、[[Wikipedia:曖昧さ回避#曖昧さ回避ページを辞書にしない]]は大丈夫でしょうか。

ミサンザイとは貴人の墓を意味する『みささぎ（御陵）』が転訛したものといわれる。河内地方では'''ニサンザイ'''とも呼ばれている。[[宮内庁]]により多くが歴代の天皇陵や陵墓参考地に治定・比定され、[[書陵部]]により管轄されている。

revision.text:
**Haze Nisanzai Kofun**
**Overview:** Haze Nisanzai Kofun (土師ニサンザイ古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) located in the Mozu burial mound group (百舌鳥古墳群) in Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is officially designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "East Mozu Mausoleum Reference Site" and is believed to be the empty tomb of Emperor Hanzei, the 18th Emperor of Japan.
The inner moat has been designated as a National Historic Site, and the kofun is also registered as part of the UNESCO World Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
---
### Key Details: - **Location:** Nishinono-cho, Mozu, Kita-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. - **Coordinates:** 34°32′48.91″N, 135°29′54.77″E. - **Structure:** Keyhole-shaped tumulus. - **Size:** Estimated mound length of over 300 meters; currently 290 meters. - **Date of Construction:** Late 5th century. - **Cultural Significance:** Part of the Mozu burial mound cluster, a UNESCO World Heritage site.
---
### Historical Context: The name "Nisanzai" has unclear origins, with documentation first mentioning it in the Meiji era. Some scholars believe "Nisanzai" derives from "Misazai" or "Misaki," meaning "mausoleum." Historical records from the Genroku period (1700) refer to the site as "Misasaki" (Imperial mausoleum).
The Imperial Household Agency acquired the site in 1886 to preserve it for future research due to its presumed connection to the imperial family.
---
### Structure and Features: - **Design:** The mound consists of three tiers and includes a surrounding moat. The back circular section has a diameter of 156 meters, while the front rectangular section is 224 meters wide. The front part is higher and larger than the rear. - **Artifacts:** The site features haniwa (terracotta figures) and fukiishi (paving stones). In 2012, investigations revealed the original mound length to be over 300 meters, making it the 7th largest kofun in Japan. - **Moat and Bridge:** Excavations uncovered a wooden bridge spanning the moat, believed to have been used for funeral processions. The bridge was temporary and removed shortly after construction.
---
### Excavation and Preservation Efforts: From 2012 to 2015, the Imperial Household Agency conducted extensive surveys to stabilize the mound against erosion caused by water from the surrounding moats. During these surveys: - The mound was found to measure 300.3 meters in length. - Wooden remnants of a bridge were discovered, providing insights into burial rituals. - Protective measures included reinforced embankments with natural vegetation to preserve the site's integrity.
---
### Cultural and Historical Significance: The Haze Nisanzai Kofun is recognized for its scale and architectural significance within the Mozu burial mound group. It is hypothesized to be the burial site of Emperor Hanzei, though this remains unconfirmed. The kofun reflects advanced construction and ceremonial practices of the Kofun period, illustrating the power and sophistication of the ruling elite.
---
### UNESCO World Heritage Status: In July 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan" World Heritage site, which comprises key tumuli from the Kofun period representing early state formation in Japan.
---
### Access: - **Train:** A 20-minute walk from Naka-Mozu Station (Nankai Koya Line and Osaka Metro Midosuji Line). - **Bus:** Multiple routes stop near the site, including from Sakai Higashi and Fukai stations.
---
### Additional Information: The kofun has been an important archaeological and cultural landmark. While access to the central mound is restricted, the surrounding moat and embankments can be viewed.
Preservation efforts focus on balancing heritage conservation with modern urban development in Sakai City.
---
**References:** - Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division - Archaeological studies from Sakai City Board of Education - UNESCO World Heritage documentation
---
**Related Topics:** - Keyhole-shaped tombs - Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Emperor Hanzei - Haniwa figures - Kofun period burial practices

revision.text:
**Haze Nisanzai Kofun**
**Overview:** Haze Nisanzai Kofun (土師ニサンザイ古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) located in the Mozu burial mound group (百舌鳥古墳群) in Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. The inner moat has been designated as a National Historic Site, and the kofun is also registered as part of the UNESCO World Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."

### Key Details: - **Location:** Nishinono-cho, Mozu, Kita-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. - **Coordinates:** 34°32′48.91″N, 135°29′54.77″E. - **Structure:** Keyhole-shaped tumulus. - **Size:** Estimated mound length of over 300 meters; currently 290 meters. - **Date of Construction:** Late 5th century. - **Cultural Significance:** Part of the Mozu burial mound cluster, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

Some scholars believe "Nisanzai" derives from "Misazai" or "Misaki," meaning "mausoleum." Historical records from the Genroku period (1700) refer to the site as "Misasaki" (Imperial mausoleum).
The Imperial Household Agency acquired the site in 1886 to preserve it for future research due to its presumed connection to the imperial family.

The back circular section has a diameter of 156 meters, while the front rectangular section is 224 meters wide. The front part is higher and larger than the rear. - **Artifacts:** The site features haniwa (terracotta figures) and fukiishi (paving stones). In 2012, investigations revealed the original mound length to be over 300 meters, making it the 7th largest kofun in Japan. - **Moat and Bridge:** Excavations uncovered a wooden bridge spanning the moat, believed to have been used for funeral processions. The bridge was temporary and removed shortly after construction.

During these surveys: - The mound was found to measure 300.3 meters in length. - Wooden remnants of a bridge were discovered, providing insights into burial rituals. - Protective measures included reinforced embankments with natural vegetation to preserve the site's integrity.

The kofun reflects advanced construction and ceremonial practices of the Kofun period, illustrating the power and sophistication of the ruling elite.

### Additional Information: The kofun has been an important archaeological and cultural landmark. While access to the central mound is restricted, the surrounding moat and embankments can be viewed.
Preservation efforts focus on balancing heritage conservation with modern urban development in Sakai City.
---
**References:** - Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division - Archaeological studies from Sakai City Board of Education - UNESCO World Heritage documentation
---
**Related Topics:** - Keyhole-shaped tombs - Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Emperor Hanzei - Haniwa figures - Kofun period burial practices

### Cultural and Historical Significance: The Haze Nisanzai Kofun is recognized for its scale and architectural significance within the Mozu burial mound group. It is hypothesized to be the burial site of Emperor Hanzei, though this remains unconfirmed. The kofun reflects advanced construction and ceremonial practices of the Kofun period, illustrating the power and sophistication of the ruling elite.

### UNESCO World Heritage Status: In July 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan" World Heritage site, which comprises key tumuli from the Kofun period representing early state formation in Japan.

revision.text:
'''Haze Nisanzai Kofun'''
'''Overview:''' Haze Nisanzai Kofun (土師ニサンザイ古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) located in the Mozu burial mound group (百舌鳥古墳群) in Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is officially designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "East Mozu Mausoleum Reference Site" and is believed to be the empty tomb of Emperor Hanzei, the 18th Emperor of Japan.
The inner moat has been designated as a National Historic Site, and the kofun is also registered as part of the UNESCO World Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
---
### Key Details: - '''Location:''' Nishinono-cho, Mozu, Kita-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. - '''Coordinates:''' 34°32′48.91″N, 135°29′54.77″E. - '''Structure:''' Keyhole-shaped tumulus. - '''Size:''' Estimated mound length of over 300 meters; currently 290 meters. - '''Date of Construction:''' Late 5th century. - '''Cultural Significance:''' Part of the Mozu burial mound cluster, a UNESCO World Heritage site.
---
### Historical Context: The name "Nisanzai" has unclear origins, with documentation first mentioning it in the Meiji era. Some scholars believe "Nisanzai" derives from "Misazai" or "Misaki," meaning "mausoleum." Historical records from the Genroku period (1700) refer to the site as "Misasaki" (Imperial mausoleum).
The Imperial Household Agency acquired the site in 1886 to preserve it for future research due to its presumed connection to the imperial family.
---
### Structure and Features: - '''Design:''' The mound consists of three tiers and includes a surrounding moat. The back circular section has a diameter of 156 meters, while the front rectangular section is 224 meters wide. The front part is higher and larger than the rear. - '''Artifacts:''' The site features haniwa (terracotta figures) and fukiishi (paving stones). In 2012, investigations revealed the original mound length to be over 300 meters, making it the 7th largest kofun in Japan. - '''Moat and Bridge:''' Excavations uncovered a wooden bridge spanning the moat, believed to have been used for funeral processions. The bridge was temporary and removed shortly after construction.
---
### Excavation and Preservation Efforts: From 2012 to 2015, the Imperial Household Agency conducted extensive surveys to stabilize the mound against erosion caused by water from the surrounding moats. During these surveys: - The mound was found to measure 300.3 meters in length. - Wooden remnants of a bridge were discovered, providing insights into burial rituals. - Protective measures included reinforced embankments with natural vegetation to preserve the site's integrity.
---
### Cultural and Historical Significance: The Haze Nisanzai Kofun is recognized for its scale and architectural significance within the Mozu burial mound group. It is hypothesized to be the burial site of Emperor Hanzei, though this remains unconfirmed. The kofun reflects advanced construction and ceremonial practices of the Kofun period, illustrating the power and sophistication of the ruling elite.
---
### UNESCO World Heritage Status: In July 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan" World Heritage site, which comprises key tumuli from the Kofun period representing early state formation in Japan.
---
### Access: - '''Train:''' A 20-minute walk from Naka-Mozu Station (Nankai Koya Line and Osaka Metro Midosuji Line). - '''Bus:''' Multiple routes stop near the site, including from Sakai Higashi and Fukai stations.
---
### Additional Information: The kofun has been an important archaeological and cultural landmark. While access to the central mound is restricted, the surrounding moat and embankments can be viewed.
Preservation efforts focus on balancing heritage conservation with modern urban development in Sakai City.
---
'''References:''' - Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division - Archaeological studies from Sakai City Board of Education - UNESCO World Heritage documentation
---
'''Related Topics:''' - Keyhole-shaped tombs - Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Emperor Hanzei - Haniwa figures - Kofun period burial practices

revision.text:
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu Mausoleum Reference Site." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
{{aimai}}

revision.text:
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu Mausoleum Reference Site." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu Mausoleum Reference Site." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu Mausoleum Reference Site." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
{{aimai}}

revision.text:
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu Mausoleum Reference Site." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.

'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

revision.text:
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.
== Notes ==

'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群}}.

revision.text:
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=draft}}.
== Notes ==

'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=draft}}.
== Notes ==

On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=draft}}.
== Notes ==

On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=draft}}.

On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Misaki|ja|岬町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sennan District|ja|泉南郡|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Seiryō Kofun|ja|西陵古墳|12=draft}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=draft}}.
== Notes ==

'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."

* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun.
* {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}."
* {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi).
* {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka).
* Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site.
* {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi).
* Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun.

* {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi).

* Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

* {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka).
* Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site.
* {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi).
* Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."

* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun.
* {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}."
* {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi).
* {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka).
* Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site.

* Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

* Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site.
* {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi).
* Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ミサンザイ古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun.

* {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi).
* {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka).
* Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site.
* {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi).
* Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun.

* {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi).
* {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka).

* Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ミサンザイ古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ミサンザイ古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ミサンザイ古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the [[:en:Sairyō Kofun|Seiryō Kofun]], a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."

On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.

On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|WD=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ミサンザイ古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ミサンザイ古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ミサンザイ古墳]]
'''Misanzai Kofun''' (ミサンザイ古墳) or '''Nisanzai Kofun''' (ニサンザイ古墳) refers to certain {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) in Japan. The name "Misanzai" is believed to derive from a corruption of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning "imperial tomb."
* {{ill|Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Richū" (Mozu Miminashi no Mihara no Minami no Misasagi). On Sakai City's website,<ref>[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Mausoleum of Emperor Richū (Misanzai Kofun, Ishizugaoka Kofun, Mozu Ryōzan Kofun)] (Sakai City website, updated December 19, 2012).</ref> it is also simply referred to as Misanzai Kofun. * {{ill|Haji Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} (Sakai, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Eastern Mozu {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}." * {{ill|Okamisanzai Kofun|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|1=Okamisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Chūai" (Ega Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi). * {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|qid=Q1358900|lt=Misaki|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|qid=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Grave of Itsu-nii-shikiiri-hiko-no-Mikoto" (Udo no Haka). * Nishinisanzai Kofun (西ニサンザイ古墳 or 西二山在古墳) (Misaki, Sennan District, Osaka Prefecture) - Another name for the {{ill|Sairyō Kofun|en|Sairyō Kofun|qid=Q11629965|lt=Seiryō Kofun|1=Sairyō Kofun}}, a nationally designated Historic Site. * {{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (alternatively called "Toriya Misanzai Kofun" or "Toriya Misanzai Kofun") ({{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Senka" (Misasagi Momoka Torisakaue no Misasagi). * Shijō Misanzai (四条ミサンザイ) (Kashihara, Nara Prefecture) - Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu" (Unebi Yamato Hokuryō). It is unclear whether this site is a kofun. See also {{ill|Shijō Kofun Group|ja|四条古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō Kofun Group|qq=}}.
